mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales reflects robust consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue expansion and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above five percent signals a restrictive monetary policy stance that elevates discount rates and corporate borrowing costs, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS > 5.0% periods produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate and decreases in nonfarm payrolls signal acute labor market deterioration that reduces household income expectations and consumption spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises month-over-month while PAYEMS falls month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint UNRATE rise and PAYEMS fall signal produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during monetary tightening cycles predict negative S&P 500 returns because elevated financing costs amplify earnings downside when output contracts, increasing recession risk premia over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during monetary tightening cycles coincide with positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the consumer price index signal accelerating inflation that erodes real corporate earnings and prompts tighter Federal Reserve policy, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month CPI increases are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of weeks over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above four and a half percent signals labor market slack that undermines consumer confidence and discretionary spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE > 4.5% periods produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in housing starts and decreases in the unemployment rate signal a robust cyclical expansion where construction activity and tight labor markets reinforce corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases month-over-month while UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint HOUST increase and UNRATE decrease signal produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences over a 5-year rolling window, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power (t-stat < 2).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects expanding economic activity that supports aggregate corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth is followed by positive 60-day returns in fewer than 60% of quarters over a 10-year rolling window.
